demographic_description: Voters who prioritize deficit reduction and are relatively open to spending cuts.
psychographic_description: Concerned about debt levels and more accepting of painful trade-offs.
behavioral_description: Less protective of federal programs if cuts are framed as deficit reduction.

question_results.question_text: Reduce federal funding for programs that help lower income Americans.
Gradually raise the age at which people can begin receiving Social Security benefits.
Reduce federal funding for education.
Limit tax deductions for interest paid on home mortgages.
Raise the tax rate on investment income.
Reduce Medicare benefits for seniors with higher incomes.
Reduce military defense spending.
Reduce federal funding for college student loan programs.
Limit tax deductions for large corporations.
Raise the income tax rate on incomes over $250,000 a year.
Reduce federal funding for scientific research.

demographic_description: Middle-of-the-road voters who prefer deficit reduction through higher taxes on affluent households and corporations.
psychographic_description: Want fiscal restraint but resist sharp cuts to education and social supports.
behavioral_description: More likely to back progressive revenue solutions than benefit cuts.

question_results.question_text: Reduce federal funding for programs that help lower income Americans.

Reduce military defense spending.
Reduce federal funding for college student loan programs.

Reduce federal funding for scientific research.

demographic_description: Voters who strongly protect education, Medicare, student loans, and anti-poverty programs.
psychographic_description: View deficit reduction through social cuts as unfair or harmful.
behavioral_description: More willing to support tax increases than reductions in social spending.
